question_text: Verify the address of the emergency. What is the exact address?
What is the callback number you are calling from?
Tell me exactly what happened.
Is s/he conscious?
Is s/he breathing normally?
Where exactly is the pain? Can you point to it?
When did the pain start?
Did the pain come on suddenly or gradually?
On a scale of 1 to 10, how severe is the pain?
Is the pain constant or does it come in waves?
Has s/he vomited? How many times? Is there any blood in the vomit?
Is there any blood in the stool or has s/he had black, tarry stools?
Does s/he have diarrhea?
When was the last bowel movement?
Is the abdomen rigid or hard to the touch?
Does s/he have a fever?
Is there any chance s/he could be pregnant?
Has s/he had any abdominal surgeries in the past?
Does s/he have a history of kidney stones, gallstones, or ulcers?
Is s/he diabetic?
Has s/he eaten anything unusual or potentially contaminated?
Is there any pain in the chest or back associated with the abdominal pain?
Is s/he able to keep fluids down?
Does s/he feel dizzy or lightheaded when standing?
ECHO-level: Unconscious with abdominal distension and not breathing
DELTA-level: Rigid abdomen with signs of shock — pale, clammy, rapid pulse
DELTA-level: Vomiting large amounts of blood or passing large bloody stools
DELTA-level: Severe abdominal pain with syncope
DELTA-level: Suspected ruptured ectopic pregnancy — lower abdominal pain, positive pregnancy, lightheaded
condition: pregnancy AND lower abd pain AND dizziness

question_text: CHARLIE-level: Severe pain (>=8/10) with rigid abdomen, conscious
condition: severe pain AND rigid abdomen AND conscious

question_text: CHARLIE-level: Abdominal pain with fever >101F and unable to keep fluids down
condition: pain AND high fever AND vomiting

question_text: BRAVO-level: Moderate pain with vomiting but no blood, keeping some fluids
condition: moderate pain AND vomiting AND no bleeding

question_text: BRAVO-level: Known kidney stone pattern — severe flank pain, writhing
condition: flank pain AND hx kidney stones

question_text: ALPHA-level: Mild to moderate abdominal pain, no vomiting, no bleeding, stable
condition: mild pain AND no red flags

question_text: ALPHA-level: Mild abdominal discomfort with diarrhea, able to keep fluids down
condition: mild pain AND diarrhea AND tolerating fluids

question_text: OMEGA-level: Chronic abdominal discomfort, no new symptoms, physician follow-up planned
condition: chronic AND stable AND follow-up arranged

question_text: Have the patient lie in a position of comfort — many prefer on their side with knees drawn up.
Do NOT give the patient anything to eat or drink.
Do NOT give any pain medication — this may mask symptoms the paramedics need to assess.
If the patient is vomiting, turn them on their side to prevent choking.
If the patient becomes unconscious and stops breathing, begin CPR.
Gather all medications and have them ready for the paramedics.
Stay on the line. Help is on the way.
If anything changes — if s/he gets worse or stops breathing — call us back immediately.
